text.title: Nginx Configuration Hardening Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real nginx-configuration deployments, each paired with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: Output contains placeholder tokens or empty required fields.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped post-fill validation step before commit.
antipattern.fix: Run the validator script — exit-code 1 blocks commit; fill every required field before re-run.

antipattern.symptom: Change applied across all environments instead of the intended one.
antipattern.root-cause: Methodology applied without environment scoping in inputs.
antipattern.fix: Always pass --env / scope; reject runs missing the scope flag.

antipattern.symptom: Recommendation references metrics older than the configured freshness window.
antipattern.root-cause: Cached inputs not refreshed before the methodology ran.
antipattern.fix: Validate input timestamps; fail-fast if any input is older than the methodology's documented window.
